original: I've searched and can't find anything on here similar to this...-- -- I've been on IVIG for 10+ years, no major reactions but just switched to SubQ for insurance and scheduling reasons. I had my first treatment yesterday and I was miserable. I had 13 grams on 4 sites. It took almost 4 hours. When the nurse stuck one of the needles in I think she got one of my ab muscles so everytime I tensed up I would get a sharp pain.-- -- I'm super skinny so I think part of the problem was that I don't really have much subcutaneous volume for the medicine to go into.-- -- Waking up this morning I don't have any of the side effects that I've read other people on here talking about, headaches etc, but it feels like I was punched in the gut over and over until I was all bruised up.-- -- I want to give the Sub Q at least a month before I say I'm switching back to IVIG but it's pretty hard to rationalize 4 hours each week and soreness for atleast 24 hours as being worth it.-- -- Anyone else have similar experience?-- -- I'm on Gammagard (if that makes a difference)
translated: ဒီမှာ ဒီလိုမျိုးနဲ့ဆင်တူတာကို ရှာမတွေ့ဘူး...-- -- ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ IVIG ကို ၁၀ နှစ်ကျော် သုံးစွဲခဲ့ပြီး ဘာဆိုးရွားတဲ့ တုံ့ပြန်မှုမှ မရှိခဲ့ပါဘူး။ ဒါပေမယ့် အာမခံနဲ့ အချိန်ဇယား အဆင်ပြေလို့ SubQ ကို ပြောင်းလိုက်ပါတယ်။ မနေ့က ပထမဆုံး ကုသမှုခံယူခဲ့ပြီး အရမ်းခံရခက်ပါတယ်။ ၁၃ ဂရမ်ကို နေရာ ၄ ခုမှာ ထိုးခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ၄ နာရီနီးပါး ကြာပါတယ်။ သူနာပြုဆရာမက ဆေးထိုးအပ်တစ်ချောင်းကို ထိုးတဲ့အခါ ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ ဝမ်းဗိုက်ကြွက်သားထဲကို ထိုးမိတယ်ထင်တယ်။ ဒါကြောင့် ကြွက်သားတွေကို တင်းလိုက်တိုင်း စူးရှတဲ့ နာကျင်မှုကို ခံစားရပါတယ်။-- -- ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မက အရမ်းပိန်တော့ ပြဿနာရဲ့ တစ်စိတ်တစ်ပိုင်းက ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မမှာ တကယ်တမ်း...

original: hi i been on steriods on and of for 3 monts to do wit some kind of allerige ive been taking stomach tablets but sometimes i feel in going to choke plus terrible stomach pain i waiting to see a specialist thanks
translated: မင်္ဂလာပါ၊ ကျွန်တော် အလာဂျီတစ်မျိုးကြောင့် Steroid ဆေးကို ၃ လလောက် သောက်လိုက်၊ နားလိုက် လုပ်နေပါတယ်။ အစာအိမ်ဆေးပြားတွေ သောက်နေပေမယ့် တစ်ခါတလေမှာ အသက်ရှူကျပ်တော့မလို ခံစားရပြီး အစာအိမ်လည်း အရမ်းနာကျင်ပါတယ်။ ကျွမ်းကျင်တဲ့ဆရာဝန်နဲ့ ပြဖို့ စောင့်နေပါတယ်။ ကျေးဇူးတင်ပါတယ်။

original: My anxiety started the beginning of April. Went to my doctor and was told it was vertigo because at that point I was just feeling dizzy and off balance. As days passed, I began to have panic attacks and increased anxiety. My general doctor prescribed me Xanax to take as needed. Even after only taking it a few times I realized with out any meds I would have constant panic attacks and started to become agoraphobic and left my job. Finally went to a psychiatrist who kept me on the Xanax but also on 10mg Prozac. Prozac was horrible for me I couldn't eat or sleep, my anxiety was even worse, I was having more panic attacks, throwing up and my fear of going out got even worse. She then switched me to Zoloft 25mg. My first week was good. I still felt anxious and panicky but was able to leave my house. I'm now about 3 weeks in and I'm feeling as if I'm almost back where I started. Every day I wake up dizzy and shaky, my anxiety goes up and down through out the day and I've barely left my house. I'm starting to think SSRI meds are not for me. I'm debating maybe trying an SNRI. Was just hoping some people can share what meds helped them or didn't help them and how they dealt with their anxiety. I feel like I'm at my breaking point.
translated: ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက ဧပြီလအစမှာ စတင်ခဲ့တာပါ။ ဆရာဝန်ဆီသွားတော့ ခေါင်းမူးပြီး ကိုယ်ကိုမထိန်းနိုင်ဖြစ်နေလို့ ခေါင်းမူးရောဂါလို့ ပြောပါတယ်။ ရက်တွေကြာလာတာနဲ့အမျှ ထိတ်လန့်တုန်လှုပ်တဲ့ ဝေဒနာတွေနဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေ ပိုတိုးလာပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ အထွေထွေရောဂါကုဆရာဝန်က လိုအပ်ရင် သောက်ဖို့ Xanax ဆေးကို ညွှန်းပေးပါတယ်။ ဆေးကို အနည်းငယ်သာ သောက်ပြီးတဲ့နောက်မှာတောင် ဘာဆေးမှမသောက်ရင် ထိတ်လန့်တုန်လှုပ်တဲ့ ဝေဒနာတွေ အမြဲခံစားရမယ်ဆိုတာကို သဘောပေါက်လာပြီး လူအများနဲ့ ဝေးဝေးနေချင်စိတ်တွေ ဖြစ်လာကာ အလုပ်ပါ ထွက်လိုက်ရပါတယ်။ နောက်ဆုံးတော့ စိတ်ရောဂါအထူးကုဆရာဝန်ဆီ သွားပြတော့ Xanax ဆေးကို ဆက်သောက်ခိုင်းပြီး...

original: I have been really under the weather and exhausted. My mom went in for surgery a week ago for an aneurysm and came home only to have to go back into ICU. Her COPD and heart went nuts because of a medicine they had her on, but she is stabilizing. I was up with her the whole night and totally broke down because I was not well enough to take care of her. -- -- Taking care of her and doing and running to the hospital, etc has totally done me in. I am in so much physical body pain along with needing to sleep constantly but not being able to because I feel on ALERT constantly because of her situation. She calls me very early in the AM and I have begun to turn my phone off at night, because I need sleep and yet I am so anxious. -- -- I had IVIG a week ago, but I feel like the IGG's are gone. I am so tired. Tomorrow I am going to the immunologist to ask if it's possible if stress can literally make the IGG's go away. I feel like I am burning the candle at both ends and I have nothing left. The day after I had the IVIG, I had such energy it was great...but now....nothing. -- -- I know that after she gets home, I am going to fall into my bed and fall into a coma. I told her last night, that I can't do this anymore. That something has to give. My brother is helping, but I live closest to her and the hospital. I told him that if anything else goes on with her or goes wrong, she will have to go into assisted living. I cannot do this anymore. I feel like my life is revolving around the fires I am constantly putting out on her behalf.-- -- I would love to write an article about how parents really need to think about the whole nursing home situation and not expect their kids to drop everything to take care of them. It really makes no sense to me. A lot of people feel that "My parents had me, so I have to take care of them" and parents feel the same way. I am not of the same thought process. You had me, I didn't ask you to....so don't expect that my life should be about taking care of you..SORRY! Babies are easier than parents, especially if you have a difficult, talk back parent who wants your help, but then freaks out if you take over too much. It sucks. They know they are forgetful, but God forbid if you tell them that. STRESS. -- -- I also just found out that a medicine that has been helping my Fibromyalgia and that I was receiving for free, I will no longer be able to get anymore for free because the generic just came out. Lidoderm patches help me a lot. The irony is that the patches that are generic are only $5.00 less than the brand name. The pharmacist was appalled by that. So, it will be $270 a month, but I don't have that in my budget. So, I am trying to figure out what to do about that. -- -- Life is hard and not just for me. Look at all of the people who's insurances are not covering their IVIG...it breaks my heart. It's sad. -- -- I needed this rant...I am losing steam and the ability to get things done. I feel brain dead, I have such malaise and I am just spent. Physically and emotionally spent.
translated: ကျွန်မ တော်တော်လေး နေမကောင်းဖြစ်ပြီး အားအင်ကုန်ခမ်းနေပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ တစ်ပတ်က အမေက ဦးနှောက်သွေးကြောဖောင်းခြင်း (aneurysm) အတွက် ခွဲစိတ်မှုခံယူခဲ့ပြီး အိမ်ပြန်ရောက်ပြီး မကြာခင်မှာပဲ အထူးကြပ်မတ်ကုသဆောင် (ICU) ကို ပြန်သွားရပါတယ်။ သူ့ရဲ့ COPD (နာတာရှည် အဆုတ်ရောဂါ) နဲ့ နှလုံးက သူသောက်နေတဲ့ ဆေးကြောင့် ဖောက်ပြန်သွားပေမယ့် အခုတော့ တည်ငြိမ်နေပါပြီ။ ကျွန်မ သူ့ကို တစ်ညလုံး ပြုစုပေးခဲ့ပြီး သူ့ကို ဂရုစိုက်ဖို့ ကျွန်မကိုယ်တိုင် မကျန်းမာတဲ့အတွက် စိတ်ဓာတ်ကျဆင်းသွားခဲ့ပါတယ်။ သူ့ကို ပြုစုစောင့်ရှောက်ပေးခြင်း၊ ဆေးရုံကို အမြဲသွားလာနေရခြင်း စတာတွေက ကျွန်မကို လုံးဝပင်ပန်းနွမ်းနယ်စေပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မမှာ ရုပ်ပိုင်းဆိုင်ရာ နာကျင်ကိုက်ခဲမှုတွေ အများကြီးခံစားနေရပြီး...

original: Hi everyone, As per my title it has been nearly a month since I increased my dose to 20mg, I had planned on totally quitting drinking but I let myself down as after 2 weeks I had quite a few drinks and did so on 3 occasions the most recent being saturday night just gone. The feelings when drinking seem to be pressure headache increased and slight pain in my temples, just feeling like my head is swollen or something it is hard to explain all of this tightness around my whole head, I have had this quite a while but it seems to be worse if I consume alcohol. I was on 10mg for a few mths before increasing and I have never had this, I also feel at times my anxiety is a lot worse, last night I felt completely anxious, I am actually wondering are these tabs just not working on me. I could really do with some advice please, I do not plan on drinking again but by drinking did I mess it all up, I have no idea.
translated: အားလုံးပဲ မင်္ဂလာပါ။ ခေါင်းစဉ်မှာဖော်ပြထားတဲ့အတိုင်း ကျွန်တော့်ဆေးပမာဏကို 20mg အထိ တိုးမြှင့်ခဲ့တာ တစ်လနီးပါးရှိပါပြီ။ အရက်လုံးဝဖြတ်ဖို့ စီစဉ်ထားပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော့်ကိုယ်ကျွန်တော် အားမရဖြစ်ခဲ့ရပါတယ်။ ဘာလို့လဲဆိုတော့ ၂ ပတ်အကြာမှာ ကျွန်တော် အတော်များများသောက်ခဲ့ပြီး အဲဒီလို ၃ ကြိမ်ဖြစ်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ နောက်ဆုံးတစ်ကြိမ်ကတော့ စနေနေ့ညကပါ။ အရက်သောက်တဲ့အခါ ခံစားရတာတွေကတော့ ခေါင်းကိုဖိထားသလို ခံစားရခြင်း၊ ခေါင်းကိုက်ခြင်းနဲ့ နားထင်တွေမှာ အနည်းငယ်နာကျင်ခြင်းတို့ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ခေါင်းတစ်ခုလုံး ဖောင်းအစ်နေသလို ခံစားရပြီး ခေါင်းတစ်ခုလုံး တင်းကျပ်နေတာကို ရှင်းပြဖို့ ခက်ခဲပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော် ဒီလိုခံစားနေရတာ...

original: I started Citalopram 20mg yesterday, when will my sleep and appetite return. I have no hunger and feeling sick. God I hope this works soon.
translated: မနေ့က စီတာလိုပရမ် (Citalopram) ၂၀ မီလီဂရမ် (20mg) စသောက်ပါတယ်။ ဘယ်အချိန်မှာ အိပ်ချိန်မှန်ပြီး အစာစားချင်စိတ် ပြန်ဖြစ်လာမလဲ။ အခု ဗိုက်မဆာသလို ပျို့အန်ချင်သလို ခံစားနေရတယ်။ မြန်မြန်သက်သာပါစေလို့ ဆုတောင်းပါတယ်။

original: Hi all. Been doing really well on sertaline 50mgs for 5 weeks and last night I had the most horrific sleep. Thoughts racing hot flushes and now I'm really anxious today. Please help as I thought I was doing so well. How can I get a good sleep? I take my meds at night on docs advice.  Feel so bad and have an empty nest as kids back at school
translated: အားလုံးပဲ မင်္ဂလာပါ။ Sertraline 50mg ကို ၅ ပတ်သောက်ပြီး အခြေအနေတွေ တော်တော်ကောင်းလာပါတယ်။ မနေ့ညကတော့ အိပ်မက်ဆိုးတွေ မက်ပြီး အိပ်လို့မရခဲ့ပါဘူး။ အတွေးတွေက ပြေးလွှားနေပြီး ကိုယ်တွေလည်း ပူလာပါတယ်။ ဒီနေ့မှာတော့ စိုးရိမ်စိတ်တွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်နေပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မ အခြေအနေတွေ ကောင်းလာပြီလို့ ထင်ထားတာကြောင့် ကူညီပေးကြပါဦး။ ဘယ်လိုလုပ်ရင် ကောင်းကောင်း အိပ်လို့ရမလဲ။ ဆရာဝန်ညွှန်ကြားချက်အရ ဆေးကို ညဘက်သောက်ပါတယ်။ ကလေးတွေ ကျောင်းပြန်သွားကြတော့ အိမ်ကလည်း တိတ်ဆိတ်ပြီး စိတ်မကောင်းဖြစ်နေပါတယ်။

original: I have been feeling low for awhile and decided to see the doctor. She upped my Citalopram from 20mg to 40mg. It's been about three weeks now and my mood has not lifted. If anything I feel anxious and worried especially about money and lack of it. I get panicky at night so was not sleeping but now have sleeping pills to help. I feel like I am letting every one down especially as I have other help problems and waiting to find out if I have rheumatoid arthritis. I need to work and mentally and physically unable to but need to get better quick. Everyone is looking forward to Christmas and I am dreading it! I have loss urge to eat as well. Any advice appreciated.
translated: ကျွန်မဟာ ခံစားမှုမကောင်းတာ ကြာနေပြီဖြစ်လို့ ဆရာဝန်နဲ့ သွားတွေ့ဖို့ ဆုံးဖြတ်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ဆရာဝန်က ကျွန်မရဲ့ Citalopram ဆေးပမာဏကို 20mg ကနေ 40mg ထိ တိုးပေးခဲ့ပါတယ်။ အခု ၃ ပတ်လောက်ရှိပြီဖြစ်ပေမယ့် ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိတ်အခြေအနေက မတက်လာသေးပါဘူး။ တကယ်လို့ တစ်ခုခုဆိုရင် စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်ပြီး အထူးသဖြင့် ငွေကြေးနဲ့ ငွေကြေးမလုံလောက်မှုတွေအတွက် စိတ်ပူနေပါတယ်။ ညဘက်တွေမှာ ထိတ်လန့်လာတတ်လို့ အိပ်မပျော်နိုင်ပေမယ့် အခု အိပ်ဆေးတွေ သောက်နေရပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မမှာ တခြားကျန်းမာရေးပြဿနာတွေလည်းရှိနေပြီး အထူးသဖြင့် rheumatoid arthritis ရှိမရှိကို စောင့်ဆိုင်းနေရလို့ လူတိုင်းကို စိတ်ပျက်စေတယ်လို့ ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မ အလုပ်လုပ်ဖို့ လိုအပ်ပြီး စိတ်ပိုင်းဆိုင်ရာရော ရုပ်ပိုင်းဆိုင်ရာရော...

original: Hi there everyone! I have not been here for quite some time. --I've worsened over the last 8 mos (was in remission for first time last summer - BLISS to feel NORMAL for first time in 7 yrs!)!! --Since I've tried every other med and it seems only thing I can tolerate (or that works) is Prednisone, the Rheumy decided to try Humira. I've taken 2 injections so far but do not feel any better at all. --In all honesty, I'm pretty sure the FRIGID COLD of Fargo is doing me in! We are seriously thinking about moving but my husband has to find a position (he is a University administrator) and those are few and far between! But in my heart, I KNOW it's the cold that's doing this to me (worsening it, at least!) and NOW we are gearing up for our 4th record-breaking FLOOD in the 5 yrs since we've been here! I'm beginning to think it's our luck! If we didn't have BAD LUCK.., We'd have NO LUCK at all!!! --Anyhow.. Have anyone tried Humira and if so, what luck (uh... Maybe wrong word!) -how about this - have u had good results??! And what of side effects?! I am honestly scared to death of this! --Thank you for your time! Angelina
translated: မင်္ဂလာပါ အားလုံး! ကျွန်တော် ဒီကို မရောက်တာတော်တော်ကြာပြီ။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၈ လအတွင်းမှာ ကျွန်တော့်အခြေအနေက ပိုဆိုးလာတယ် (ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့နွေရာသီမှာ ပထမဆုံးအကြိမ် သက်သာသွားခဲ့တယ် - ၇ နှစ်အတွင်း ပထမဆုံးအကြိမ် ပုံမှန်ခံစားရတာက အရမ်းပျော်စရာကောင်းတယ်!)။ တခြားဆေးတွေအကုန်လုံးကို ကျွန်တော်စမ်းပြီးပြီဆိုတော့ ကျွန်တော်ခံနိုင်ရည်ရှိတဲ့ (ဒါမှမဟုတ် အလုပ်ဖြစ်တဲ့) တစ်ခုတည်းသောအရာက Prednisone ဖြစ်ပုံရတယ်၊ ဒါကြောင့် Rheumy က Humira ကိုစမ်းဖို့ ဆုံးဖြတ်လိုက်တယ်။ ကျွန်တော် အခုထိ ၂ ကြိမ်ထိုးပြီးပြီ ဒါပေမယ့် ဘာမှမသက်သာသေးဘူး။ ရိုးရိုးသားသားပြောရရင် Fargo ရဲ့ အရမ်းအေးတဲ့ရာသီဥတုက ကျွန်တော့်ကို ဒုက္ခပေးနေတယ်လို့ ထင်တယ်။ ကျွန်တော်တို့ всерьёз စဉ်းစားနေတာက...

original: Hi, I'm new here and hoping to find someone who can relate to me.  I've suffered with anxiety for about 12 years now. I was doing daily well until maybe 2 years ago. My son was sick and mom had dementia and it was a bad time. Mom passed last year, son is doing well but my anxiety has only gotten worse and I feel it's out of control. I am constantly feeling like I'm dying and that there's something wrong with my heart. I feel impending doom all of the time now. I just don't know what to do anymore. I'm on Effexor XR and Ativan. I've been tapering off of Effexor cause it makes my anxiety worse. My psychiatrist gave me Prozac to switch to but I'm afraid of switching. Before I had started on Effexor I went through a few SsRI's all with bad effects, especially with Zoloft (it made me suicidal). I feel so lost and alone
translated: မင်္ဂလာပါ၊ ကျွန်မ ဒီကို ပထမဆုံးရောက်ဖူးတာပါ။ ကျွန်မနဲ့ခံစားချက်တူတဲ့သူကို ရှာတွေ့မယ်လို့ မျှော်လင့်ပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မဟာ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုဝေဒနာကို ၁၂ နှစ်လောက် ခံစားခဲ့ရပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၂ နှစ်လောက်အထိ နေ့တိုင်းကောင်းကောင်းနေနိုင်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မရဲ့သားလေး နေမကောင်းဖြစ်ခဲ့ပြီး အမေကလည်း မှတ်ဉာဏ်ချို့ယွင်းတဲ့ရောဂါ (dementia) ဖြစ်နေတော့ အဲဒီအချိန်က အရမ်းဆိုးခဲ့ပါတယ်။ အမေက ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့နှစ်က ဆုံးသွားခဲ့ပြီး သားလေးကတော့ သက်သာနေပါပြီ။ ဒါပေမယ့် ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက ပိုဆိုးလာပြီး ထိန်းချုပ်လို့မရတော့ဘူးလို့ ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ ကျွန်မဟာ အမြဲတမ်းသေတော့မလိုလို၊ နှလုံးမှာ တစ်ခုခုမှားနေသလိုလို ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ အခုဆိုရင် အမြဲတမ်း မကောင်းဆိုးဝါးတွေ ကျရောက်တော့မလို ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ ဘာလုပ်ရမှန်းကို မသိတော့ပါဘူး...

original: Hi my name is Steve and I suffer from Chronic idiopathic urticaria mixed with Pressure Urticaria, with a side order of Delayed pressure urticaria. Oh and potential H Pylori also!----I have suffered for 7 or 8 years now and am affected each and every day. the last time i can remember a pain free time was for about a week back in 2007 - thankfully during my honeymoon!----I have tried many different medications over the years. Currently I am taking Xyzal and Azathioprine. Previously I was taking a combination of Dapsone, Xyzal, Cyklokapron and Singulair. This combination helped for a period of approx. 1 year but then lost its effect.So my specialist put me on the Azathioprine and Xyzal combination along with a steroid course of Prednisolone. I don't take the steroids any more for one simple reason. I ended up in hospital for 5 days because of it. I was admitted and given 3 massive doses of Morphine to counteract the pain this drug caused my in my abdomen. I have to have tests now to see what damage has been done as there has definitely been a change in my stomach. ----I have also been on Atarax, and a few other drugs in the past - none of which worked.----The C.I.U. causes me to suffer daily. 50% of the days I can lives with it but the other 50% I am forced to lay on the sofa and do not much. ----The delayed pressure urticaria causes me to suffer every evening depending on what I have done during the day. If I go for a walk longer than 10mins my feet will swell in a very painful way. If I sit on a hard backed chair for more than 30mins my back becomes a mine field of swellings. no-matter how soft the chair I sit in the backs of my thighs will swell almost every evening. Even laying on the sofa cause my back to swell up badly. ----the pressure urticaria causes an extremely painful swelling soon after any knocks. for example i knocked my knee on a cupboard and within 30mins my leg had locked tight and refused to bend due to the swelling surrounding my knee.----I have to have blood tests taken every week now as the Azathioprine can cause a drop in the white blood cell count and potentially lead to cancer.----I have to go for a C Urea breath test to find out if I have h pylori.----my daily life has been ruled by this condition for too long. I am constantly aware of how I am sitting standing or walking. My wife is extremely frustrated due to the fact we can not do the things any normal married couple does. We love long walks but I cant do it. We love going to the cinema but rarely go due to the 2 hours of sitting still. We are trying for a baby but cant try as often as we would like due to me either suffering or being aware of the pain I will feel the next day due to the pressure on certain parts of my body. it inst nice having your genitals swell to 3 times their normal flaccid size!!!----Anyway enough moaning from me. i just found this group and though I would get that out of my system as no one around me here truly understands what I go through on a daily basis. My wife tries to understand but gets extremely frustrated at it all and I have given up telling her about my illness! lol----Anyway - cheers for listening - laters
translated: ကျွန်တော့်နာမည် စတိဗ်ပါ၊ နာတာရှည် အကြောင်းရင်းမသိတဲ့ အင်ပျဉ်ရောဂါ (Chronic idiopathic urticaria) နဲ့ ဖိအားကြောင့်ဖြစ်တဲ့ အင်ပျဉ်ရောဂါ (Pressure Urticaria) ကို ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ နောက်ပြီး နောက်ကျမှပေါ်တဲ့ ဖိအားကြောင့်ဖြစ်တဲ့ အင်ပျဉ်ရောဂါ (Delayed pressure urticaria) လည်းရှိနိုင်ပါတယ်။ အို... နောက်ပြီး H. Pylori လည်းဖြစ်နိုင်သေးတယ်! ---- ကျွန်တော်ဟာ ၇ နှစ် ဒါမှမဟုတ် ၈ နှစ်လောက်ကြာအောင် ခံစားနေရပြီး နေ့တိုင်းလိုလို ထိခိုက်ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ နောက်ဆုံး ကျွန်တော်မှတ်မိနိုင်တဲ့ နာကျင်မှုမရှိတဲ့အချိန်က ၂၀၀၇ ခုနှစ်က တစ်ပတ်လောက်ပါ - ကံကောင်းထောက်မစွာနဲ့ ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ပျားရည်ဆမ်းခရီးတုန်းကပေါ့! ---- ကျွန်တော်ဟာ နှစ်တွေတစ်လျှောက် ဆေးအမျိုးမျိုးကို စမ်းသောက်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ လက်ရှိမှာ Xyzal နဲ့ Azathioprine ကို သောက်နေပါတယ်။ အရင်တုန်းကတော့...

original: To explain, I need to start at the beginning. About six months ago, I had a secure friend group, but did not feel comfortable around them, or anyone else for that matter (apart from my family). I spent the majority of my life revolving around them, worrying about what I should or should've said, how I should act, and stressed so much of the time. Looking back on it, I feel like it was because I didn't feel good enough about myself. Then, they moved to a location on the x ground where I would have to intentionally travel to to go to them, instead of the usual place near me. This is where my problems really started. I ended up spending the majority of my time on my own, not feeling like I could go and talk to them. I felt like something was wrong with me, and so I became my own doctor. Every day, I would reflect on my emotions thoughts and actions, and compare them to what I thought everyone else did, I went over lots of topics: Did I have low-self esteem? Was I too conscious of my actions? Was I too self reflective? Did I have too much self doubt? Was I just weird? The list goes on forever. Jump to 3 months later, now. I sometimes go meet with my friends, and then just leave them when they go to the playground etc.. To do homework, or just pretend to be doing something on my own. The solution I thought would work, was too simply eradicate any thought that I believed to be "bad". I began to concentrate on the world right in front of me, and remove any thought that entered into my head. Then, I realised that by blinking and by "wiping my mind" at the same time, I could cut loose any thought that was inside my head. Whilst this worked sometimes, I came to the realisation a few days ago that no one can be confident all the time, or always be sure of themselves. So, I rooted the problem down this:"Analyse of my conscious process". What this means is that every thought i had, I would not only be aware of it, but I would judge whether it was good or bad or weird, and then depending on the answer, I would get rid of it or keep it. What this lead to was a habit that I could not wish more I could be rid of. Subconsciously, I now completely blank out every time I blink, every time I move my eyes, and every time I think, I automatically wipe my mind. This has lead to a state of complete blankness, I have no thoughts at all. This was because I had not realised what I was actually doing when I was wiping my mind. Instead of getting rid of the thought by concentrating on something else, I was diverting my brains attention to my sense of hearing. So right now, when I am completely "blank" , I am not thinking, I am only taking in what I can hear. If I close my eyes their is no change in my sense, and noises do not change volumes, it as if I am only using my hearing sense. What I would like to know is if anyone has done this before? And what I can do to stop it, because I know the source of problem now, but because have completely killed all of my thoughts, imagination and emotion, I can not think about what I need to do. Please help!
translated: ရှင်းပြဖို့ဆိုရင် အစကနေစရမယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ခြောက်လလောက်က ကျွန်တော့်မှာ ခိုင်မာတဲ့ သူငယ်ချင်းအုပ်စုရှိခဲ့ပေမယ့် သူတို့နဲ့ပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ်၊ တခြားဘယ်သူနဲ့ပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ် (မိသားစုဝင်တွေကလွဲလို့) သက်တောင့်သက်သာမရှိဘူး။ ကျွန်တော့်ဘဝရဲ့ အများစုကို သူတို့ပတ်လည်မှာပဲ လည်ပတ်နေခဲ့တယ်။ ဘာပြောသင့်လဲ၊ ဘယ်လိုပြုမူသင့်လဲဆိုတာကို စိုးရိမ်ပြီး အချိန်အများကြီး ဖိစီးမှုတွေနဲ့ကုန်ဆုံးခဲ့တယ်။ အဲဒီအချိန်တွေကို ပြန်ကြည့်လိုက်တော့ ကျွန်တော့်ကိုယ်ကျွန်တော် လုံလောက်တယ်လို့ မခံစားရလို့လို့ ထင်မိတယ်။ ပြီးတော့ သူတို့က x မြေပြင်ပေါ်က နေရာတစ်ခုကို ပြောင်းရွှေ့သွားကြပြီး ကျွန်တော်...

original: Cant stop shaking inside not sure why last 2 weeks i had the best 2 weeks of my life .. then monday started crying n anxiety started up real bad.. its been everyday since then.. i thought i was getting better n i was getting my life back.. why would this happen ? translated: အတွင်းထဲက တုန်လှုပ်နေတာကို မရပ်တန့်နိုင်ဘူး။ ဘာကြောင့်လဲဆိုတာ သေချာမသိဘူး။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၂ ပတ်က ကျွန်မဘဝရဲ့ အကောင်းဆုံး ၂ ပတ်ပါပဲ။ ပြီးတော့ တနင်္လာနေ့မှာ ငိုဖို့စပြီး စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေက တကယ်ဆိုးဆိုးရွားရွား စဖြစ်လာတယ်။ အဲဒီနောက်ပိုင်း နေ့တိုင်းဖြစ်နေတယ်။ ကျွန်မ သက်သာလာပြီလို့ ထင်ခဲ့ပြီး ကျွန်မဘဝကို ပြန်ရတော့မယ်လို့ ထင်ခဲ့တယ်။ ဘာလို့ ဒီလိုဖြစ်ရတာလဲ။ ကျွန်မ ဘာအမှားလုပ်မိလဲဆိုတာကို ရှာဖွေဖို့ ကြိုးစားနေတယ်။ ဒါကို ပြင်ဖို့ ကျွန်မရဲ့စိတ်က အပြိုင်အဆိုင် တွေးတောနေတယ်။ ကျွန်မ အရမ်းစိတ်ဖိစီးနေလို့ နေ့တိုင်း အများကြီး ငိုနေတယ်။ ကျွန်မရဲ့ ကုထုံးက မကြာခင်မှာ ပြီးဆုံးတော့မယ်။ ကျွန်မ တက်ရောက်နေတဲ့ အစီအစဉ်လည်း ပြီးဆုံးတော့မယ်။ ပြီးတော့ ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိတ်ရောဂါအထူးကုဆရာဝန်ကလည်း ၃ ပတ်ရှိပြီအထိ ကျွန်မကို ပြန်မဆက်သွယ်သေးဘူး။ ကျွန်မမှာ...

original: There doesn't seem to be much activity on this board - but I would be interested to hear from anyoneelse who finds it hard to come off Prozac.   Apologies in advance for the long post.  My last attempt to come off Prozac lasted almost a year.   I have tried so hard...but I am finally admitting defeat.  I am going to ask for a new prescription tomorrow.  It feels like this little capsule that twenty years ago was my saviour has now become my captor.  It is with feeling of utter failure and slight despair that I take these pills again.  I feel I have no choice.   The worst thing is, I don't understand why I hate it so much.     I was on Prozac for almost twenty years until I weaned myself off almost a year ago.  This was one of many attempts.   During those twenty years, I was never completely comfortable taking it.    I was grateful for how it worked, how it changed my life, but for some reason that I could never shake off, I  just didn't like the the idea of being on medication every day.   I was not at ease with the idea of having to be on a prescription -  of being dependent on this little green and cream capsule -  simply to feel normal like everyone else.    My GP could never understand when I talked about coming off it.  He would more or less say, its working for you - why change things?  Just take it, and forget about it.   I still  don't understand why I am so uncomfortable about taking it.     I thought in the beginning, that I would be cured of my depression and anxiety, and go back to the happy person I was,   then when I was 'fixed' I would stop taking it.  I was told then it was not 'addictive', and it WAS only for the short term.   So how come, every time I came off...I not only suffered the most awful symptoms..I also felt 100 times worse than I did before I started taking it?   It is like Prozac has changed my brain, so I am dependent on it simply to have any quality of life.     With Prozac, I am relatively content, I enjoy socialising, I can run a house and 'look after' my family and my ageing parents.  Simply, I just get on with my life which is a good one.    Without Prozac, I am anxious and irritable all of the time.  I feel far, far worse than I EVER did in the before I went on on it.  I thought I was depressed then....but from what I remember it was never as bad as this.  Its hard to explain, but it is like it is self fulfilling... like Prozac itself is causing my mental health problems.  I panic at the thought of having to do anything that involves social evenings, sometimes I can't even cope with trivial or ordinary things like organising meals, or  making lists.  It all seems too overwhelming so I just don't do it.   I get completely worked up about nothing.  I fly into rages and feel awful afterwards. I wake in the morning with a nervous tummy and terrible anxiety about the day.  When I physically get up and get on with it I feel better...but I can lie for an hour in bed in the morning feeling sick with nerves, and cannot find a 'place' to go in my head that is pleasant.  It is always doom and gloom, and anger and sadness....and recently I have been contemplating all sorts of ways of leaving it all behind me.   I can't live like this any more.    I guess it is just a case of getting my head to the point where I can see Prozac as a friend and not the enemy.  I guess I blame it for getting me where I am  in the first place -  totally dependent on it.  It's like, I have no choice in the matter.  This is not how I usually live my life :p.    I feel defeated and overpowered, even though the outcome is to my benefit.  Its hard to explain.  I mean, what if they find out it causes tumours, or my doctor just decides to stop prescribing it down the line.  I feel trapped, no matter how I look at it.      I guess I would tell anyone thinking of going onto this drug that it is like a pitcher plant.  Once you are in, you are in it forever no matter how much you try to scramble up the sides.  It is not just a case of take it till you get well, then thank it,  and move on.   Prozac has you in its grip forever.   From my experience.. I would say only start it if you can accept the fact it probably WILL be for life.

original: Hi All, So I have found this forum extremely helpful for me and am turning to it for advice. I started on only 10mg of Citalopram (I know this is a low dose) for severe anxiety that I have been dealing with for the past 7 months. I thought I would be able to manage the anxiety on my own but could not. I started the Citalopram 16 days ago, and currently take it around 8pm at night. First day was a little rough, but after that, the side effects for me were not bad at all. On day 8, 9, and 10 I had really good days and since being on Citalopram my anxiety has reduced a lot. For the past couple of days though I have been feeling really down and unmotivated and a little foggy brained. I have never really experienced depression before but am feeling really down. I understand that I will have good days and bad days until the meds fully kick in and even then will experience some blips. I just need a little reassurance on this process. Has anyone else experienced depression or feeling down when first starting this medication? Will these feelings eventually go away? Thank you all for reading and any advice given!

translated: မကြာသေးမီက ကျွန်တော့်နှလုံးက အိပ်ရာကထတာနဲ့ ထိုင်တာနဲ့တင် အရမ်းမြန်နေတယ်။ ထလိုက်တာနဲ့ အသက်ရှူရခက်သလို ခံစားရပြီး နှလုံးခုန်နှုန်းက အရမ်းမြန်လာတယ်။ တစ်နေ့ကို အကြိမ်များစွာဖြစ်တတ်ပြီး နေ့တိုင်းလိုလို ဖြစ်နေတယ်။ နှလုံးအထူးကုဆရာဝန်နဲ့ပြပြီး စမ်းသပ်မှုတွေ အများကြီးလုပ်ခဲ့တယ်။ ၇ ရက် နှလုံးစောင့်ကြည့်စက်၊ ပြေးစက်ပေါ်မှာ စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုစမ်းသပ်ခြင်း၊ နှလုံးအာထရာဆောင်း၊ သိုင်းရွိုက် အားလုံးပုံမှန်ပဲ။ သူက ကျွန်တော့်မှာ arrhythmia နည်းနည်းရှိတယ်၊ ဒါပေမယ့် အန္တရာယ်မရှိဘူးလို့ပြောတယ်။ ကျွန်တော်ကောင်းတယ်လို့ သူကပြောပြီး ဘာဆေးမှ မပေးဘူး။ ဒါပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော်...

original: Hi everybody, I'am having these hives that drive me crazy during the night and I have to endure them for about 4 hours starting at 2am till 6am. They appear at random spots on my skin and they disappear quickly in about 15 minutes then other ones appear and the process gets repeated for about 4 hours. I've never been allergic to anything in my life. It all started a month ago when I ate a pizza later that night (2am) I had hives but it wasn't as intense as what I have now. two weeks later, I changed my diet so I could gain weight so I started eating 4 meals a day (plus workout).I started drinking alot of milk on the days when I workout usually 3 or 4 times a week 1/2 liter plus weight gainer (serious mass) before workout and the other half plus weight gainer after workout.That night I couldn't sleep till 6am. So The next day I didn't drink milk nor weight gainer, but I still had hives at night. So I checked what I ate that day It was all usual except fish. Today, I ate nothing but meat, rice, salad, grapes and other fruit. And late at night I had some hives but it wasn't very intense (8 or 9 hives). I'am desperately trying to gain weight, I normally gain weight so hardly although I eat alot and sit all day. Now, I have doubled my efforts, and I'am gaining weight so slowly.And quite frankly I'am not willing to give up now. So, please help me. I appreciate a little guidance. Thanks,
translated: အားလုံး မင်္ဂလာပါ၊ ကျွန်တော် ညဘက်မှာ အရမ်းယားယံတဲ့ အင်ပျဉ်တွေထနေလို့ အရမ်းဒုက္ခရောက်နေပြီး မနက် ၂ နာရီကနေ ၆ နာရီအထိ ၄ နာရီလောက် ခံစားနေရပါတယ်။ အရေပြားပေါ်မှာ ကျပန်းနေရာတွေမှာ ပေါ်လာပြီး ၁၅ မိနစ်လောက်အတွင်းမှာ မြန်မြန်ပျောက်သွားကာ တခြားအင်ပျဉ်တွေ ထပ်ပေါ်လာပြီး ဒီဖြစ်စဉ်က ၄ နာရီလောက် ထပ်ခါထပ်ခါ ဖြစ်နေပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်ဘဝမှာ ဘာနဲ့မှ ဓာတ်မတည့်ဖူးပါဘူး။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ တစ်လလောက်က စဖြစ်တာပါ။ အဲဒီညက (မနက် ၂ နာရီ) ပီဇာစားပြီးတော့ အင်ပျဉ်တွေထွက်လာပေမယ့် အခုလောက်တော့ မပြင်းထန်ပါဘူး။ နှစ်ပတ်အကြာမှာတော့...

original: ive been taking citalopram for about 15 months and on 40mg for about 4 months. I had the nausea for a couple of weeks and tiredness which is quite common I guess.� But in the last couple of weeks I've started having terrible night sweats /cold sweats. I was wondering if any one else has had this and after a while of taking cit ?� Any comments appreciated Thanks�
translated: ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မဟာ Citalopram ကို ၁၅ လလောက်သောက်နေခဲ့ပြီး 40mg ကို ၄ လလောက်သောက်နေပါတယ်။ ပျို့အန်တာကို ၂ ပတ်လောက်ခံစားခဲ့ရပြီး ပင်ပန်းနွမ်းနယ်တာကတော့ ဖြစ်လေ့ဖြစ်ထရှိတယ်လို့ထင်ပါတယ်။ ဒါပေမယ့် လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၂ ပတ်လောက်ကစပြီး ညဘက်ချွေးထွက်လွန်တာ/ချမ်းစိမ့်ချွေးတွေထွက်လာပါတယ်။ တခြားသူတွေလည်း ဒီလိုဖြစ်ဖူးလား၊ Citalopram သောက်ပြီးခဏအကြာမှာဖြစ်တာလားဆိုတာ သိချင်ပါတယ်။ ဘာမှတ်ချက်ပေးချင်တာပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ် ကျေးဇူးတင်ပါတယ်။

original: Hey everyone. Not sure where to start. A year ago I had a near death experience. around October, I experienced some mild anxiety (was told it was bertigo) and dismissed it. As the months went on, I noticed my anxiety getting a tiny bit worse. For example in February I went out for drinks for my birthday and after having one was feeling very anxious (I never had this issue). In March, my grandma passed away. About two weeks later, I started getting panic attacks every few days to the point where I had to leave my job and can barely leave my house. My GP gave me Xanax since I was in his office during a random panic attack (was there for a sore throat and had a panic attack in the waiting room). Finally got in with a psychiatrist 2 weeks ago and was given Prozac 10mg along with continuing the Xanax. The Prozac made me even worse. My anxiety was so bad I did not leave my home for 2 weeks and missed important family events. I am now starting Zoloft and very on edge. I'm afraid of having the horrible experience I had on the Prozac which was a major increase in anxiety, throwing up, no appetite, frequent panic attacks, and not sleeping. Just wondering if anyone can share their experiences with anxiety medication and if it helped or not. I don't want to keep trying out meds if nothing will help and only seems to make me feel worse.
translated: ဟေ့လူတိုင်း။ ဘယ်ကစရမလဲမသိဘူး။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့တစ်နှစ်က သေလုမြောပါးအတွေ့အကြုံတစ်ခုရှိခဲ့တယ်။ အောက်တိုဘာလောက်မှာ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုအနည်းငယ် (ခေါင်းမူးတယ်လို့ပြောတယ်) ခံစားခဲ့ရပြီး လျစ်လျူရှုထားခဲ့တယ်။ လတွေကြာလာတာနဲ့အမျှ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက နည်းနည်းပိုဆိုးလာတာကို သတိထားမိတယ်။ ဥပမာ ဖေဖော်ဝါရီလမှာ မွေးနေ့အတွက် အပြင်ထွက်ပြီး တစ်ခွက်သောက်ပြီးတဲ့နောက် စိုးရိမ်စိတ်တွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်လာတယ် (ဒီလိုပြဿနာမျိုး တစ်ခါမှမဖြစ်ဖူးဘူး)။ မတ်လမှာ အဘွားဆုံးသွားတယ်။ နှစ်ပတ်လောက်အကြာမှာ ရက်အနည်းငယ်ခြားတစ်ခါ ထိတ်လန့်တုန်လှုပ်တဲ့ဝေဒနာတွေ ခံစားလာရပြီး...

original: Hey all..  I'm so depressed over my panic disorder . This all started last November n it's still going on.. I was on 225mgs of effexor this drug did nothing for me but make my anxiety 1000x worse.. not the drug for me .  I withdrew from it in about 3 weeks.. not as bad as all the Horry stories.. now j started paxil 10 mgs 3 weeks ago tomorrow.  I'm taking 2 mgs klonopin per day as well until I'm adjusted to the correct med n dose.. I had severe migraine yesterday sent me to er my limbs went numb , vision issues it was horrible.. today all day anxiety n panic.. called doctor he's upping me to 20mgs.. starting tonight.. I have no clue how I will feel.. n I have my monthly friend so that doesn't help my anxiety..okay panic is stemmed around health anxiety.. I get werid physical sensations n then I'm in a cloud of panic.. I think I'm dieing or going crazy.. no matter how hard I try to believe I'm not dieing it doesn't work my brain plays sick games on me.. when will this ever end I want my life back.. my family has suffered thru this, my finances, my job.. you have to understand I have had many people die in front of me from a drop in a hat.. this is where the underlining issue comes from. I see therapists, a psychiatrist.. I do Dbt therapy.. I don't know what else to do.. please some encouraging words would help in this fight.. thank you  I love this site you are the only people in the world I know understands what I'm going thru..
translated: အားလုံးပဲ.. ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်သောကရောဂါ (panic disorder) ကြောင့် စိတ်ဓာတ်ကျနေပါတယ်။ ဒါတွေအားလုံးဟာ ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့ နိုဝင်ဘာလက စတင်ခဲ့တာဖြစ်ပြီး အခုထိလည်း ဆက်ဖြစ်နေတုန်းပါပဲ.. ကျွန်တော် Effexor 225mg သောက်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ဒီဆေးက ကျွန်တော့်အတွက် ဘာမှအထောက်အကူမဖြစ်တဲ့အပြင် ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေကို အဆ ၁၀၀၀ ပိုဆိုးစေခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ဒါက ကျွန်တော့်အတွက် သင့်တော်တဲ့ဆေးမဟုတ်ပါဘူး။ ကျွန်တော် ၃ ပတ်အတွင်းမှာပဲ ဆေးဖြတ်လိုက်ပါတယ်။ ကြောက်စရာကောင်းတဲ့ အဖြစ်အပျက်တွေလောက်တော့ မဆိုးပါဘူး။ အခု Paxil 10mg ကို မနက်ဖြန်ဆို ၃ ပတ်ပြည့်ပါပြီ။ မှန်ကန်တဲ့ဆေးနဲ့ ဆေးပမာဏကို ညှိယူချိန်အထိ Klonopin 2mg ကို တစ်နေ့ ၂ ကြိမ် သောက်နေပါတယ်။ မနေ့က ခေါင်းကိုက်ဝေဒနာ (severe migraine) ပြင်းထန်စွာခံစားရလို့ အရေးပေါ်ခန်း (ER) ကို သွားခဲ့ရပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ လက်တွေ ခြေတွေ ထုံကျင်သွားပါတယ်။

original: I'm a newbie here after some reassurance, been on citalopram for several years but since January I've had increased stress and this resulted in weekly panic attacks. Doc put me up to 30mg 4wks ago and I thought I was getting there but panic attack again returned. So a wk ago doc upped me to 40mg, and now I'm wrackedwith anxiety and panic worrying if I'll ever shake this off. Please someone tell me I will get back to me again soon, I so want to be there for my family.
translated: စိတ်အေးချမ်းမှုရဖို့ ဒီကိုရောက်လာတဲ့ လူသစ်တစ်ယောက်ပါ။ Citalopram ကို နှစ်ပေါင်းများစွာ သောက်ခဲ့ပေမယ့် ဇန်နဝါရီလကစပြီး စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုတွေ များလာလို့ တစ်ပတ်ကို Panic Attack တစ်ခါဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဆရာဝန်က လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၄ ပတ်က 30mg တိုးပေးလိုက်ပြီး အဆင်ပြေလာပြီလို့ ထင်ခဲ့ပေမယ့် Panic Attack က ပြန်ဖြစ်လာပြန်တယ်။ ဒါကြောင့် လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ တစ်ပတ်က ဆရာဝန်က 40mg ထပ်တိုးပေးလိုက်တော့ စိုးရိမ်စိတ်တွေနဲ့ Panic တွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်နေပြီး ဒီအခြေအနေကနေ လွတ်မြောက်နိုင်ပါ့မလားလို့ စိတ်ပူနေပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော် ပုံမှန်အတိုင်း ပြန်ဖြစ်လာမယ်လို့ တစ်ယောက်ယောက် ပြောပေးပါဦး။ ကျွန်တော့်မိသားစုအတွက် အားကိုးရာဖြစ်ချင်ပါတယ်။

original: Hey everyone. Well I am now going into my 6th week of taking 20mg of citalopram. I've noticed some changes and the last panic attack I had was about a week & a half ago. I feel a lot more comfortable going out and confident I can control my anxiety. Some of the side effects I was experiencing have decreased where as others I still notice such as my skin feeling as if it's burning, light headed and also a dry mouth. Although I am feeling a lot better then I did I still have concerns.. I've been having this horrible feeling of nervousness in the pit of my stomach which rises up by my lungs, when his happens I start to feel very hot. Which then kicks off anxiety.. All of my panic attacks and anxiety started because I thought I had something wrong with my lungs. Then I would think something was wrong with my heart. I'm constantly fearing I have something seriously wrong with me. Some days i don't think about it at all but these past 2 days I have started this way of thinking again.. This feeling of nervousness rising to my lungs is getting me worried. Surely all of these strange things I'm feeling can't just be made up in my head? Is there something wrong? Or could it just be the tablets. Because I sure know I didn't feel like this before.. before the panic attacks and citalopram. When will this all just vanish ..� Thanks for reading� X�
translated: မင်္ဂလာပါ အားလုံး။ ကောင်းပါပြီ၊ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ Citalopram 20mg ကိုသောက်တာ ၆ ပတ်မြောက်ထဲကို ရောက်နေပါပြီ။ ပြောင်းလဲမှုတချို့ကို သတိထားမိပြီး နောက်ဆုံး Panic Attack ဖြစ်တာက တစ်ပတ်ခွဲလောက်ရှိပါပြီ။ အပြင်ထွက်ရတာ ပိုအဆင်ပြေပြီး စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုကို ထိန်းချုပ်နိုင်တယ်လို့ ယုံကြည်မှုရှိပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ ခံစားခဲ့ရတဲ့ ဘေးထွက်ဆိုးကျိုးတချို့က လျော့နည်းသွားပေမယ့် အရေပြားက ပူလောင်သလိုခံစားရတာ၊ ခေါင်းမူးတာနဲ့ ပါးစပ်ခြောက်တာတွေကိုတော့ သတိထားမိနေတုန်းပါပဲ။ အရင်ကထက် အများကြီးပိုကောင်းတယ်လို့ ခံစားရပေမယ့် စိုးရိမ်မှုတွေတော့ ရှိနေပါသေးတယ်။

original: Since the end of November 2016, I've been having really bad anxiety. I fear going to college every night and always experience high stress throughout lessons to the point where I find it difficult to concentrate and sometimes have to leave (I have left 3 lessons before, left a mock exam twice and simply haven't attended a lesson last Friday (27th January) because I was too scared. I have to wait a week and a half to see my doctor and get severe anxiety when I talk to the doctor when I'm there. I feel like my parents and teachers are getting sick of me leaving which scares me further and I'm worried about taking Lorazepam, which I have only had once but is the only thing I can think of that might get me through the week. I don't know if I'm going to be able to cope through the week without it. I feel like I'm going to be sick during lessons or like the room's spinning. I keep creating new things to be afraid of. Has anyone else had to leave a situation like that before? And do I have to take something to get me through the week? How do I calm down. Thanks Annelo
translated: ၂၀၁၆ ခုနှစ် နိုဝင်ဘာလကုန်ကစပြီး စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်နေတယ်။ ညတိုင်း ကောလိပ်သွားရမှာကို ကြောက်တယ်။ သင်ခန်းစာတွေမှာ စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုအရမ်းများလို့ အာရုံစိုက်ရခက်ခဲပြီး တစ်ခါတလေ ထွက်သွားရတယ်။ (၃ ကြိမ် သင်ခန်းစာကထွက်သွားဖူးတယ်၊ စာမေးပွဲတုကို ၂ ကြိမ်ထွက်သွားဖူးပြီး ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့သောကြာနေ့ (ဇန်နဝါရီ ၂၇ ရက်) မှာ ကြောက်လွန်းလို့ သင်ခန်းစာကို မတက်ခဲ့ဘူး။ ဆရာဝန်နဲ့တွေ့ဖို့ တစ်ပတ်ခွဲလောက်စောင့်ရဦးမယ်။ ဆရာဝန်နဲ့တွေ့တဲ့အခါ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်တယ်။

original: hi, ive been described 20mg of citalopram. ive been taking them for 2 weeks now. i just dont seem to be feeling any better, infact i feel worse. i have this awful "fog" in my head where i just feel really blank and very detached from everything. is this normal and is it too early to start feeling any benefits? tia
translated: ကျွန်မကို Citalopram 20mg ပေးထားပါတယ်။ ၂ ပတ်လောက်သောက်နေပါပြီ။ ဘာမှမထူးခြားဘူး။ ပိုဆိုးလာသလို ခံစားရတယ်။ ခေါင်းထဲမှာ မှုန်ဝါးဝါးကြီးဖြစ်နေပြီး ဘာကိုမှ သိပ်မခံစားရတော့ဘူး။ ဒါက ပုံမှန်လား။ ဒါမှမဟုတ် အကျိုးသက်ရောက်မှု ခံစားရဖို့ စောနေသေးလား။ ကျေးဇူးတင်ပါတယ်။

original: Shalom everyone in my Inside Family! I'm back after being away for a few months healing from cataract surgery on both eyes!!!!! I will be taking it easy with the online activities for a bit, so if you message me & I don't answer right away, that's why. -- -- This will be short-have my screen on 150% view, and already dealing with eye strain. But the good news about that is that I am back!!! I have so much to say, so much to update....it's been literally an emotional hurricane these past few months. The cataracts are gone, and I have new lenses implants. I watched the opening ceremonies and footage/overview of Russia's landscape for the Olympics last night, and I absolutely bawled! I mean, totally snot-sobbing bawled!!! I saw colors I hadn't realized I hadn't seen in a year!!!!!! I watched Journey 2: Mysterious Island today, and the same thing! I hadn't seen the movie without the cataracts before. I saw the green lightning bugs and blue glow-in-the-dark mushrooms by the river in the camping scene, and was amazed! I've seen it several times, but with the cataracts, and I never once saw those items! Dr. Hanzlik, my eye surgeon, literally saved my eyesight, and my quality of life! Mad props! He's an expert in Central Fla for eye diseases, and he said he's never seen my type of cataracts before outside of a patient with a genetic cataract! I saw him just a year ago this month, and my symptoms popped up last June or so. He told us that he's never seen cataracts begin so suddenly, and progress so aggressively! If we had waited even a month, he said I would have become totally blind. It was just unbelievable.-- -- We also have some more understanding about my stomach issues now. What a great thing when you look at a lab order from your GI doc and see there in print the dx term they haven't actually said to you aloud! Gastroparesis. I am screwed. I am 85lbs now. No muscle mass at all {like seriously, that shows up in blood work-who knew?}. Still waiting for that CT scan to confirm for the surgeon whether the mass {lymph node} in my neck is soft or hard, benign or malignant. It's about 3cm x 3.5cm now. I know that the surgery is inevitable. But at least I can see now! I have my list of movies already together for watching while I heal from that ordeal! Trying to be positive. My latest IgG numbers are around 237. Oh, and I have to mention.....my responses to the pneumococcal & influenza types A & B vaccines....drum roll please.......it's as if I have NEVER been vaccinated at all for any of it. Ever. Thankfully, I still show antibody responses to tetanus and of all things, diphtheria! WTH?!-- -- Anyway, I got my mom to delay her visit from KY until April {or June}. She cannot see me like this. I feel like we have to get me somewhat more sorted before she comes here. Stephen and I agree that she cannot know about most of this-we did keep her in the loop for the cataracts, but definitely not the mass {not with her NHL a few years ago! She'd freak the eff out} and we haven't told her too much about the gastroparesis yet, just that I have lost a lot of weight and have trouble eating now. {Come on people-when was the last time any of you had a culinary orgasm from just one absolutely plain pancake?}-- -- I think I have touched on most everything so far. I want to thank Pam & Zen for updating my Inside Family during my time away from the boards. And Zen, thank you for text-chatting with me and keeping me sane while my eyes healed. -- -- And if anyone has any advice or ideas for dealing with a pulling/pinching ligament, I would really appreciate it. The one in my left side of my neck is really painful!-- -- Heart you all!-- Shoshi
translated: ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ Inside Family ထဲကလူတိုင်းကို ရှလုံ! ကျွန်တော်မျက်စိနှစ်လုံးစလုံးကို အတွင်းတိမ်ခွဲစိတ်ပြီး လအနည်းငယ်ကြာအောင် အနားယူပြီးနောက် ပြန်လာပါပြီ!!!!! အွန်လိုင်းလှုပ်ရှားမှုတွေကို ခဏလောက်လျှော့လုပ်သွားမှာမို့ ကျွန်တော့်ကို စာပို့ပြီး ချက်ချင်းမဖြေရင် အဲဒါကြောင့်ပါ။-- -- ဒါကတိုတိုလေးပဲဖြစ်မှာပါ- ကျွန်တော့်စခရင်ကို 150% နဲ့ကြည့်နေရပြီး မျက်စိညောင်းညာတာကို ခံစားနေရပါပြီ။ ဒါပေမယ့် အဲဒီအကြောင်းနဲ့ပတ်သက်ပြီး ကောင်းတဲ့သတင်းကတော့ ကျွန်တော်ပြန်လာပါပြီ!!! ပြောစရာတွေ၊ အပ်ဒိတ်လုပ်စရာတွေ အများကြီးရှိတယ်....ဒီလအနည်းငယ်အတွင်းမှာ စိတ်လှုပ်ရှားစရာမုန်တိုင်းတစ်ခုလိုပါပဲ...

original: Has anyone ever had IVIG or SubQ and then had unusually adverse reactions or infections that made them concerned they may have picked something up from the treatment itself? I know the meds are supposed to be carefully processed, screened, filtered, etc., and there's supposedly little risk of getting anything from them. But, since my most recent IVIG treatment, I've had: 1) the worst cold sore outbreak I've had in at least 8 years; 2) an inexplicable rash on my arms that required several days of treatment to knock back; 2) the worst flu (sinus/throat/chest) and fever that I've had in at least 15 years, which I'm still battling.-- -- I have not been traveling by air and have actually been exposed to fewer people over the holidays, given all the time off from work I've had. I've gotten plenty of rest. I'm eating well and not engaging in any sort of risky behavior. There's really no reason I can think of why I should be sicker than I've been at any time in the past several years - in fact, I would think with all the treatments I've had so far, I'd be much less susceptible to coming down with these types of infections.-- -- I'm seriously considering discontinuing treatment, given that my mild CVID already makes me a borderline case anyway (and my second opinion was surprised I was on the treatment at all). Given that the treatment did not prevent me from getting repeatedly sick this past month - more so than I've been without the treatment, in fact - I'm not sure it's even particularly beneficial. -- -- Up until the last treatment, I felt I was improving slightly. This time, I can't help but feel these recent ailments are somehow tied to that last treatment. Has anyone else ever felt like something was just not right after a particular treatment?
translated: IVIG (သွေးကြောထဲထိုးသွင်းတဲ့ ခုခံအားဂလိုဘူလင်) ဒါမှမဟုတ် SubQ (အရေပြားအောက် ထိုးသွင်းတဲ့ ခုခံအားဂလိုဘူလင်) ကို ထိုးပြီးတဲ့နောက်မှာ ပုံမှန်မဟုတ်တဲ့ ဆိုးရွားတဲ့ တုံ့ပြန်မှုတွေ ဒါမှမဟုတ် ကူးစက်ရောဂါတွေ ခံစားခဲ့ရလို့ ကုသမှုကနေ တစ်ခုခု ကူးစက်ခံရမလားလို့ စိုးရိမ်ခဲ့ဖူးတဲ့သူ ရှိပါသလား။ ဆေးတွေကို ဂရုတစိုက် စီမံထုတ်လုပ်ပြီး စစ်ဆေး၊ ဇကာချပြီးမှ ထုတ်လုပ်တယ်လို့ သိထားပါတယ်။ ပြီးတော့ အဲဒီဆေးတွေကနေ ဘာမှ ကူးစက်နိုင်ခြေ မရှိဘူးလို့လည်း ပြောကြပါတယ်။ ဒါပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ နောက်ဆုံး IVIG ကုသမှုခံယူပြီးကတည်းက ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မမှာ - ၁) အနည်းဆုံး ၈ နှစ်အတွင်း အဆိုးဆုံး နှုတ်ခမ်းအနာ ဖြစ်ပွားခြင်း၊ ၂) အကြောင်းပြချက်မရှိတဲ့ လက်မောင်းပေါ်က အဖုအပိန့်တွေ ထွက်လာပြီး ရက်ပေါင်းများစွာ ကုသခဲ့ရခြင်း...

original: Hey all, Anxiety sufferer here. Diagnosed at 17, now 32. I am on no medications because after the many years taking them I decided I didn't want to be dependant on them. My main anxiety is health anxiety. Since I was 17 I feared I would have a heart attack. Over the 15 years there's rarely a day that goes by that I don't think I'm going to die. Any pain in my body, or any issues I'm having, I automatically link to a heart problem. A twinge in my finger, a numb foot from sitting on it, post exercise pain ... I like it all to a heart problem. Ontop of this I have agrophobia and a fear of other people. I rarely leave the house - which just reinforces the anxiety. I pick the young lad up from school sometimes and it's a real struggle to get to the school, and wait for him to come out. Getting in taxi's or on busses is a no no. I could go on forever with this. It limits my life, and stops me doing things I'd like to. I've had it for so long I don't know what else to do. I've tried everything. My Dad was diagnosed with terminal Cancer in July. This put a lot of stress on me. The hospital was miles away, and I had to travel to see him. For me that was hard to do, ontop of the stress of him being ill. He died on the 4th of January, and since then anxiety has been on high alert all day, every day. I now get physical symptoms such as constant nausea, feelings of losing control and unable to control myself. I'm scared and worried about everyhing all the time. From when I wake, to when I sleep. I just had a repair guy in the house and I had to hide in another room because I can't be around other people. Tomorrow I have my Dad's funeral ... oh the joy that will be! I just can't deal with this anymore. I don't know where to turn, or what to do. It's causing depression and I'm always narky and in a down mood. My son and girlfriend are affected by this. I don't do anything anymore. I sit in the house all day, working (I work from home as a programmer). It's hard though because I can't focus a lot of the time. My attention is easily swayed by anxiety. I don't have sex often, my girlfriend has noticed. I tried to explain that I am not feeling well at the moment but she thinks it's a problem with her. I hate this so much. I feel like I'm that bad I should be in a mental hospital. They won't put me in one, apparently it's not the place for me. Well guess what ... life isn't the place for me right now. I have had negative thoughts about leaving this life. I can't talk about it much as I don't know if it's a trigger that's not allowed to be discussed on the this forum. I feel like the world is ontop of me. I have funeral tomorrow, home alone Saturday (I hate being alone), Sunday I get a rest but Monday I'm having teeth taken out (I've missed the dentist appointments for 12 months because of fear, I'll probably skip this one too), a work meeting on Tuesday. It feels like it never ends. I'm someone who has his own routine. My routine consists of sitting at my computer programming in the daytime, and making music at night. Anything outside of that routine is a real problem for me. I have Aspergers Syndrome, which probably explains that. Take me away from my comfort zone and the real problems start. Sorry for the long post. That's just the tip of the iceburg.��� My upcoming appointments
translated: အားလုံးပဲ မင်္ဂလာပါ။ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မက စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှု ဝေဒနာရှင်တစ်ယောက်ပါ။ အသက် ၁၇ နှစ်မှာ ရောဂါအမည်တပ်ခံခဲ့ရပြီး အခု အသက် ၃၂ နှစ်ရှိပါပြီ။ ဆေးတွေကို နှစ်ပေါင်းများစွာ သောက်ပြီးတဲ့နောက် ဆေးပေါ်မှာ မှီခိုမနေချင်တော့လို့ ဘာဆေးမှ မသောက်တော့ပါဘူး။ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ အဓိက စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက ကျန်းမာရေးနဲ့ ပတ်သက်တဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုပါ။ အသက် ၁၇ နှစ်ကတည်းက နှလုံးဖောက်ပြန်မှာကို ကြောက်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ၁၅ နှစ်တာကာလအတွင်းမှာ သေတော့မယ်လို့ မထင်တဲ့ရက်ဆိုတာ ရှားပါတယ်။ ခန္ဓာကိုယ်မှာ နာကျင်တာပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ်၊ ပြဿနာတစ်ခုခုရှိတာပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ် နှလုံးနဲ့ပတ်သက်တဲ့ ပြဿနာလို့ အလိုအလျောက် ဆက်စပ်တွေးတောမိပါတယ်။ လက်ချောင်းလေး နာကျင်တာပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ်၊ ထိုင်နေလို့ ခြေထုံတာပဲဖြစ်ဖြစ်၊ လေ့ကျင့်ခန်းလုပ်ပြီး...

original: 4 months ago I accidentally overdosed on nortryptoline whilst trying to get some sleep (took 5 20mg) I was drunk at the time, but never the less the following day I was in A&E with palpitations and other horrible side effects. For following month I couldn't really sleep having palpitations and felt really ill, after that month I just started feeling really dozy like I was drunk 24/7 and had this tightness feeling in my chest that would come and go, I just thought it was like anxiety from that awful month and I would get heartburn 3 nights a week at average, I just continued life as normal. I went out for drinks here and there didn't make me feel too bad I actually felt in less of a daze after a drink. my diet recently had been pretty bad take away and rubbish just comfort food , so after feeling run down for so long I went to the doctor he gave me some anti anxiety meds and gave me a blood test. I must admit I do feel a little better but I still feel really tired and have pain in my abdomen and back and I seem to be drinking allot more water because I have an almost constant dry mouth and more recently I've taken note of foamy urine I've been looking up symptoms to do with liver problems so it has me some what worried just recently I've received a call from the doctors about the blood tests showing abnormal liver function" and been told to book another blood test in 3 months
translated: လွန်ခဲ့သော ၄ လခန့်က အိပ်ပျော်ရန်ကြိုးစားရင်း nortriptyline (20mg ၅ လုံး) ကို မတော်တဆ အလွန်အကျွံသောက်မိခဲ့သည်။ ထိုအချိန်က အရက်မူးနေသော်လည်း နောက်တစ်နေ့တွင် ရင်တုန်ခြင်းနှင့် အခြားဆိုးရွားသော ဘေးထွက်ဆိုးကျိုးများကြောင့် အရေးပေါ်ဌာနသို့ ရောက်ခဲ့သည်။ နောက်လတွင် ရင်တုန်ခြင်းကြောင့် ကောင်းကောင်းအိပ်မပျော်နိုင်ဘဲ တကယ်နေမကောင်းဖြစ်ခဲ့သည်။ ထိုလပြီးနောက် ကျွန်တော်သည် တစ်နေ့ ၂၄ နာရီလုံးလုံး အရက်မူးနေသလို ထိုင်းမှိုင်းလာပြီး ရင်ဘတ်ထဲတွင် တင်းကျပ်သလို ခံစားရကာ ခဏခဏဖြစ်တတ်သည်။ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုကြောင့်ဖြစ်သည်ဟု ထင်ခဲ့သည်။

original: Hello all, I have the worst health anxiety ever. I have no idea how to overcome it. i went back on my zoloft which thank god I did. every time I get an ache or a pain I always relate it to a heart attack or a stroke. is there anything to do to possibly get rid of this? I have what i think is heart burn. i'm burping so much and i feel the gas bubbles inside my chest. My chest feels weird, it doesn't really hurt it feels like a gassy feeling. and my left shoulder& arm feel sort of achy. I can't tell if it's from my anxiety or if I have heart problems.. I can breathe fine and I don't even feel dizzy. and my stomach also hurts and I get sweaty and start shaking. I just had my cbc tested 2 weeks ago doctor said everything was great& i had a bunch of tests done on my heart back in february and the cardiologist told me I had nothing to worry about. But i get aches and pains everyday. I don't get it?
translated: အားလုံး မင်္ဂလာပါ။ ကျွန်တော့်မှာ ကျန်းမာရေးစိုးရိမ်စိတ် အဆိုးဆုံးရှိတယ်။ ဘယ်လိုကျော်လွှားရမလဲဆိုတာ မသိဘူး။ ကျွန်တော် Zoloft ကို ပြန်သောက်နေတယ်။ ကျေးဇူးတင်စရာပဲ။ ကျွန်တော် နာကျင်ကိုက်ခဲတာ ဒါမှမဟုတ် နာကျင်တာတစ်ခုခုဖြစ်တိုင်း နှလုံးဖောက်တာ ဒါမှမဟုတ် လေဖြတ်တာနဲ့ အမြဲဆက်စပ်နေတယ်။ ဒါကို ဖယ်ရှားဖို့ တစ်ခုခုလုပ်လို့ရနိုင်မလား။ ကျွန်တော့်မှာ ရင်ပူတယ်လို့ထင်တယ်။ ကျွန်တော် လေတွေအများကြီးထွက်နေပြီး ကျွန်တော့်ရင်ဘတ်ထဲမှာ လေပူဖောင်းတွေရှိနေသလို ခံစားရတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်ရင်ဘတ်က ထူးဆန်းနေတယ်။ တကယ်မနာကျင်ဘူး။ လေတွေပြည့်နေသလို ခံစားရတယ်။ ပြီးတော့ ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ ဘယ်ဘက်ပခုံးနဲ့ လက်မောင်းက နာကျင်ကိုက်ခဲနေသလို ခံစားရတယ်။ ကျွန်တော် မပြောနိုင်ဘူး...

original: I'm curious of how long you can take anti-depressants before they stop working. I was on Zoloft for 4 weeks and it made me much happier, but evenutally over time my anxiety and depression came back in the 5th week. My doctor has now put me on 100mg I was wondering if it will be the same cycle where it will work for a bit and then i'll have to continue increasing the dosage or that the particular dosage could possibly work best and I can continue using the same dosage for a long amount of time and it's still working. Because I seriously wouldn't mind taking a drug for life.\xa0 If anyone could tell me their experinces it will be much appreicated.
translated: စိတ်ကျရောဂါပျောက်ဆေးတွေကို ဘယ်လောက်ကြာကြာသောက်လို့ရလဲဆိုတာ သိချင်ပါတယ်။ Zoloft ကို ၄ ပတ်သောက်တုန်းက ပျော်ရွှင်လာပေမယ့် နောက်ပိုင်း ၅ ပတ်မြောက်မှာ စိုးရိမ်စိတ်နဲ့ စိတ်ဓာတ်ကျတာတွေ ပြန်ဖြစ်လာတယ်။ အခု ဆရာဝန်က 100mg ပေးထားတယ်။ အရင်လိုပဲ ခဏပဲအာနိသင်ပြပြီး ဆေးပမာဏကို တိုးသောက်နေရမလား၊ ဒါမှမဟုတ် ဒီပမာဏနဲ့ပဲ အချိန်အကြာကြီး သောက်လို့ရမလားဆိုတာ သိချင်ပါတယ်။

original: Hi I would like peoples experience with withdrawal when lowering dose please. original: Okay so a year ago I had a very bad experience with cannabis where I had extreme panic attacks and I thought I died and so much happened that day even the ambulance came and everything I was traumatised so much. So after that night I was never the same. I would often get panic attacks and have constant anxiety and thoughts of death would make me so panicked I would experience sweaty hands, cold sensations, heart palpitations and extreme fear. So now that has improved a lot since last year however I could feel I was getting fatigue over time for no apparent reason and that would bring my anxiety back on. It wasn't until last month I had a viral Infection which was so bad. I mean it was really bad that I thought I had a killer disease which would kill me (again death thoughts). I went to the A&E three times in such a panic. Once thinking I had toxic shock syndrome. I mean I didn't care all I cared about was my health and I would do anything to find out what I have. Had blood tests done and it was all clear. Been to the gp like every week and they said I was fine too after taking bloods and urine samples. I also thought I may have an sti so I got checked at a sexual health clinic and again tests came out all clear.� So my viral infection started with a lump under my jaw and constipation. I then experienced night sweats, chills, disorientation, shortness of breath, a lump in my upper neck, Photophobia which was so bad it would give me migraines and I couldn't go out at night with all the lights outside, panic, fever, extreme body aches and extreme fatigue and extreme dizziness. This lasted 3 whole weeks and every time I would get all sorts of symptoms. So I would panic and go to the gp and sometimes A&E and all they would say was it's just an viral infection. Plus before my infection I was constipated and I had bowel problems too. In the 4th week my flu symptoms stopped. Such as sweats, chills, disorientation (I would get up feeling like I can't feel my body or like I'm leaving my body, it was SO weird, I would shake myself to gain consciousness or wake my sister to feel better) thankfully that has gone.� So It's the 5th week now but I'm still not well my fatigueness (which includes aching upper arms and legs) has not gone, neither have my headaches I get pressure in my head an ears still and I occasionally get dizziness and shortness of breath. I also feel a lump in my throat which the go said is normal however I don't. I mean I can't function properly anymore I get extremely exhausted after 3 hours of work where I work as a sales associate. My body feels like it's giving up on me. I'm in extreme pain even at this moment my arms are paining so bad. I've got an appointment for an abdominal scan and I want some other scans done too because at this moment I feel as if I have something which is causing all this pain and discomfort. Maybe then I'll be in peace that it's nothing serious and it's all down to my anxiety. I hope it is. However I don't know how to tackle it. I wake up during the night feeling strange, short of breath or I can hear my heartbeat beat so fast or hear a heartbeat in my head when I sleep. Yes I'm so paranoid and worried. My aches and pains are not going.� I need some help out here. Has anyone experienced something similar. Please get in contact. It would � make me feel so much better� Thank you�

original: This is going to be a little long, but I could really use some help. First of all I am a 27 year old male who has always had a clean bill of health.  I was sick about 3 months ago, and once I got over that I couldn't shake the cough. Very deep intense coughs. 2 months ago, one night I had one of these coughs and my chest instantly tightened up. Shortly after my back also began to tighten up. I sat down and it continued to get worse and worse, felt like someone had a rope around my chest and back and kept squeezing tighter and tighter.  Eventually I stood up to try to walk it off and began to get very dizzy. I ended up passing out very briefly. I remember going down, and I woke up immediately. The chest pain was then gone, and I was extremely sweaty and flush.  Went to ER, and was cleared of anything serious, but wasn't given any answers. In the next few days I had multiple ekgs, chest x ray, and a blood test, and everything came back clear. I went to cardiologist, had a stress echo test, and was told there is thing wrong with my heart.  So first question is, anybody have any idea what may have caused the chest pain followed by the passing out? Since then I have developed severe anxiety and panic attacks regarding this incident and the health of my heart. I have chest pain almost every day. I still am very convinced that something is going to happen to my heart, but it has also now become more of a nonstop anxiety no matter what I do. Checking my pulse all the time, worrying about everything, always on edge and just not feeling good overall. I also have been having multiple panic attacks per day.  I can't function, I have missed work, I'm no help to my wife with out 8 month old son. I feel like a complete failure, but can't do anything about it. I was on Xanax for a while, and that seemed to help a little bit. After I stopped using Xanax I was good for about a week, and then I had a HUGE panic attack and this past week has been the worst one yet.  I just started Zoloft a few days ago, though obviously it isn't helping yet. What can I expect with the Zoloft? Should I expect it to help? Any tips on how to get my life back? Sorry this is so long, I appreciate any help!
translated: ဒါက နည်းနည်းရှည်လိမ့်မယ်၊ ဒါပေမယ့် အကူအညီတကယ်လိုအပ်တယ်။ ပထမဦးဆုံးအနေနဲ့ ကျွန်တော်က အသက် ၂၇ နှစ်အရွယ် အမျိုးသားတစ်ယောက်ဖြစ်ပြီး အမြဲတမ်း ကျန်းမာရေးကောင်းခဲ့တယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၃ လလောက်က ဖျားခဲ့ပြီး အဲဒီကနေ သက်သာလာပေမယ့် ချောင်းဆိုးတာက မပျောက်ဘူး။ အရမ်းပြင်းထန်တဲ့ ချောင်းဆိုးတာမျိုးတွေ။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၂ လက တစ်ညမှာ အဲဒီလို ချောင်းဆိုးပြီး ရင်ဘတ်က ချက်ချင်းတင်းလာတယ်။ မကြာခင်မှာ ကျောကလည်း တင်းလာတယ်။ ထိုင်လိုက်တော့ ပိုဆိုးလာတယ်။ တစ်ယောက်ယောက်က ရင်ဘတ်နဲ့ ကျောကို ကြိုးနဲ့ချည်ထားသလို ခံစားရတယ်...

original: Hi there - I've tried Citalopram (20mg) in the past over 12 weeks and it really helped with anxiety but the drowsiness was quite severe. I used to take them in the morning and felt like a zombie all day! Does this wear off eventually or should I take them at night? Thinking of going back on them.
translated: မင်္ဂလာပါ - ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မဟာ အရင်က Citalopram (20mg) ကို ၁၂ ပတ်ကျော် သောက်ဖူးပြီး စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုအတွက် တကယ်ကို အထောက်အကူဖြစ်ခဲ့ပေမယ့် ငိုက်မြည်းတာက တော်တော်ဆိုးပါတယ်။ အရင်က မနက်ပိုင်းမှာ သောက်ခဲ့ပြီး တစ်နေကုန် ဖုတ်ကောင်တစ်ကောင်လို ခံစားခဲ့ရပါတယ်! ဒါက နောက်ပိုင်းမှာ သက်သာသွားမလား ဒါမှမဟုတ် ညဘက်သောက်သင့်သလား။ ပြန်သောက်ဖို့ စဉ်းစားနေပါတယ်။

original: Well were do i start ??? A few months ago started with a slight cough which has slowly got worse Dentist 2 month ago found a swollen gland in my neck 6 weeks ago started with cold, cough got worse, ear ache and sore throat 4 weeks ago started to become breatless, cough and tight throat Sonce then had cough and tight throat Yesterday i found a painless small ulcer looing lump on the floor of my mouth I feel like its one thing after another and each is just heightening my anxiety more and more !!!! I am awaiting my appointment at ENT but feel i will crack up before then !!!
translated: ဘယ်ကစရမလဲ ??? လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့လအနည်းငယ်က ချောင်းအနည်းငယ်ဆိုးရာက စခဲ့ပြီး တဖြည်းဖြည်း ပိုဆိုးလာပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၂ လက သွားဆရာဝန်က ကျွန်မရဲ့လည်ပင်းမှာ ရောင်နေတဲ့ဂလင်းတစ်ခုကို တွေ့ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၆ ပတ်က အအေးမိတာ၊ ချောင်းပိုဆိုးတာ၊ နားကိုက်တာနဲ့ လည်ချောင်းနာတာတွေ စဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၄ ပတ်က အသက်ရှူမဝတာ၊ ချောင်းဆိုးတာနဲ့ လည်ချောင်းတင်းတာတွေ စဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ အဲဒီအချိန်ကစပြီး ချောင်းဆိုးပြီး လည်ချောင်းတင်းနေပါတယ်။ မနေ့က ကျွန်မရဲ့ပါးစပ်အောက်ခြေမှာ နာကျင်မှုမရှိတဲ့ အနာလိုမျိုး အကျိတ်သေးသေးလေးတစ်ခုကို တွေ့ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ တစ်ခုပြီးတစ်ခု ဖြစ်နေသလို ခံစားရပြီး တစ်ခုချင်းစီက ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုကို ပိုပိုပြီး မြင့်တက်စေပါတယ် !!!

original: Okay so my anxiety for the most part has been pretty good I just turned 18 and I'm a pretty healthy male from labs and stuff, but in the past month has been awful full of ER visits and such since last night So this past month I have had very bad anxiety episodes sending me in to the hospital thinking I was having a heart problem I have palpitations (pvc's) and had a echo done all that and 6EKGS done in the past but after a while my doctor or pediatrician started to assume I needed to see a physiatrist which I thought I needed but I realized my anxiety had been situational and normal but everytime I had a bad pain in my chest id call and ask what should I do and they said it's just anxiety which I believed but idk if the pain can be caused by costronitis or something like that Well I'm the past two weeks I have been getting very very bad upper abdominal pain and I've been on an acid med For a while and I just am terrified it's something wrong with my aorta which I have no family history of and they took a listen to it with stethoscopes and said they didn't hear anything, I have had these pains for a while and all my blood work came back fine and I get pain in my back could this be a stomach ulcer I'm over thinking?? I just wish I had a image like a CT to confirm my aorta is okay could someone tell me if they think it's fine and when I called my doctor for a follow up they say the same thing they've been saying in the past I need to see a physchiatrist but I never really saw my self as having that kind of disorder but yeah they basically have given up on my health I feel and I'm worried please someone what should I do ?
translated: ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက အတော်လေးကောင်းပါတယ်။ အခုမှ ၁၈ နှစ်ပြည့်ပြီး ကျန်းမာရေးစစ်ဆေးမှုတွေအရ ကျန်းမာတဲ့ ယောက်ျားလေးတစ်ယောက်ပါ။ ဒါပေမယ့် လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ တစ်လအတွင်းမှာ အရေးပေါ်ဌာနကို ခဏခဏသွားရလောက်အောင် ဆိုးရွားခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့ညကတည်းက စပြီး ဒီတစ်လအတွင်းမှာ ရင်ဘတ်အောင့်ပြီး နှလုံးရောဂါဖြစ်နေတယ်ထင်ပြီး ဆေးရုံကိုပို့ရတဲ့အထိ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုတွေ အရမ်းဖြစ်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ နှလုံးခုန်မမှန်တာ (PVC's) တွေဖြစ်ပြီး Echo ရိုက်ဖူးတယ်။ အဲဒါတွေအပြင် ပြီးခဲ့တဲ့ရက်တွေမှာ 6 EKG လောက်ရိုက်ပြီးပါပြီ။ ဒါပေမယ့် နောက်ပိုင်းမှာ ဆရာဝန် ဒါမှမဟုတ် ကလေးအထူးကုဆရာဝန်က ကျွန်တော့်ကို စိတ်ရောဂါအထူးကုဆရာဝန်နဲ့ ပြသင့်တယ်လို့ ယူဆလာပါတယ်။

original: Anyone ever dealt with this? I have not been formally diagnosed with any anxiety disorder but I start counseling next week and I was worried it wouldn't help very much because I don't think I have GAD. Sounds silly, but the counseling I'll be getting is through my university's program and while they really do want to help, they see so many people that it's kind of a here's-what's-going-on and here's-how-to-deal-with-it situation and I was worried that I would get the same how-to-deal-with-it part as the hundreds of students they see for generalized anxiety.� I really had my doubts about generalized anxiety because while I do worry almost constantly, it's not over things like all of the work I have to do or how many people I'll end up seeing that day. I thought maybe I had health anxiety, but I have also worried incessantly about my own sudden death (I am only 20 and in near-perfect health) as well as a horrible disease or accident hurting one of my family members. Before anxiety took over my life, I also used to have panic attacks in situations where I thought gun violence could easily occur (at that time it'd been in the news frequently and I live in the US). One Saturday I was studying in the library with a few friends and in that library there is a staircase, and if you stand at the bottom of the staircase you can kind of see about the whole floor. I noticed there was a guy standing there with his hands in his pockets, headphones in, not saying anything. Just watching. I let it go for a while thinking he could've just been listening to a podcast or something. However he must have remained there for 30 minutes or so and somewhere in there, the unbelievably irrational thought that he may have been scoping out a proper time to commit and act of violence popped into my head, and then I could not get it out. I started imaging every scenario, which way I would leave, how far away I would get before I felt safe enough, etc. I actually had to get up and go for a 20 minute walk out of that room before I could come back and focus again. Later I felt really bad because I saw him working really hard on a project with other students.� That was not the only incidence of an event like that with my anxiety, and now it's only gotten worse. It surrounds mostly the idea of something awful very suddenly� happening to me or one of my family members. If I have a headache, well it's probably a tumor and not a million other things. I know that's pretty common place, but I've been doing a lot of reading and a defining feature of catastrophic anxiety is that one often reacts to their thoughts like these things have actually happened to him or her, and I can say without doubt that I do that. Has anyone had experience with catostrophic anxiety? I think this is something I have experienced my entire life, but now it is catching up to me and has taken over my everyday life.. �
translated: ဘယ်သူတွေ ဒီလိုမျိုး ခံစားဖူးလဲ။ ကျွန်တော့်ကို စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုရောဂါ (anxiety disorder) တစ်မျိုးမျိုးရှိတယ်လို့ တရားဝင် မစစ်ဆေးရသေးပါဘူး။ ဒါပေမယ့် နောက်အပတ်မှာ အကြံဉာဏ်ပေးခြင်း (counseling) စတော့မှာပါ။ ကျွန်တော့်မှာ GAD (Generalized Anxiety Disorder - အကြောင်းမဲ့ စိုးရိမ်သောက ရောဂါ) ရှိတယ်လို့ မထင်တဲ့အတွက် အဲဒါက သိပ်အထောက်အကူမဖြစ်မှာကို စိုးရိမ်နေပါတယ်။ နည်းနည်းတော့ ရယ်စရာကောင်းတယ်၊ ဒါပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော်ရမယ့် အကြံဉာဏ်ပေးခြင်း (counseling) က ကျွန်တော့်တက္ကသိုလ်ရဲ့ အစီအစဉ်ကနေ ရမှာပါ။ သူတို့က တကယ်ကူညီချင်ပေမယ့် လူအများကြီးကို တွေ့နေရတော့ ဒါက ဘာဖြစ်နေလဲ၊ ဘယ်လိုကိုင်တွယ်ဖြေရှင်းရမလဲဆိုတဲ့ အခြေအနေမျိုးဖြစ်နေတယ်။ ကျွန်တော်လည်း အဲဒီလို ကိုင်တွယ်ဖြေရှင်းနည်း (how-to-deal-with-it) သင်တန်းမျိုးပဲ ရမှာကို စိုးရိမ်နေပါတယ်။

translated: ဒီမနက်မှာ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ ပထမဆုံး Cit ဆေးပမာဏကို သောက်သုံးခဲ့ပေမယ့် ရိုးရိုးသားသားပြောရရင် မှန်ကန်တဲ့အရာကို လုပ်နေသလားဆိုတာ သေချာမသိပါဘူး။ Venlaxafine ဆေးကို 16mg လောက်အထိ လျှော့ချနေပြီး ဆေးဖြတ်တဲ့ဝေဒနာတွေက ဆိုးရွားလွန်းပါတယ်။ ပျို့အန်ခြင်း၊ ချွေးထွက်ခြင်း၊ ဗိုက်အောင့်ခြင်းနဲ့ ခေါင်းကိုက်ခြင်းတွေက အဆိုးဆုံးပါပဲ။ တစ်နေကုန် မအီမသာဖြစ်နေတဲ့ ခံစားချက်မျိုးကို ခင်ဗျားတို့သိပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ စိတ်အခြေအနေက နိမ့်ကျနေပြီး စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုကလည်း အတော်လေးမြင့်မားနေပါတယ်။ ဒီလိုမျိုး ဆက်ပြီးမခံနိုင်တော့ဘူးလို့ ခံစားရလို့ ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မကို သက်သာစေမယ့် တစ်ခုခု လိုအပ်ပါတယ်။ တကယ်တော့ အားလုံးလိုချင်တဲ့ ဆုလာဘ်တစ်ခုခုကို ပေးဖို့ပါပဲ...

If I was to rate the pain out of 10 it's like a 2 or 3 so it's not a bad Pain I'm assuming it could also be muscle strain
translated: ဒါကြောင့် တနင်္ဂနွေနေ့က ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ ညီနဲ့ကားထဲမှာရှိနေတုန်း ရင်ဘတ်အလယ်ပိုင်းနားမှာ နာကျင်မှုအနည်းငယ်ခံစားရပြီး ဘယ်ဘက်ရင်သားဘက်ကိုပိုပြီးနာလာပါတယ်။ ပြီးတော့ နှလုံးဖောက်ပြန်တယ်လို့ထင်ပြီး အရမ်းစိုးရိမ်လာတာကြောင့် အခြေအနေကပိုဆိုးသွားပါတယ်။ ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မ ညီက နှလုံးဖောက်ပြန်တာမဟုတ်ဘူး၊ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုနဲ့ ထိတ်လန့်မှုကြောင့်ဖြစ်တာလို့ပြောပါတယ်။ တကယ်တော့ အဲဒီနေ့က စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုများတဲ့နေ့တစ်နေ့ဖြစ်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ပြီးတော့ ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ စိတ်တည်ငြိမ်သွားပါတယ်။ မနေ့ကတော့ အဆင်ပြေပါတယ်။ မနေ့က တနင်္ဂနွေနေ့ထက်ပိုပြီး စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုများတဲ့နေ့တစ်နေ့ဖြစ်ခဲ့ပေမယ့် ဒီညမှာတော့...

original: I don't get it, I've had no real stress lately but since Wednesday my anxiety symptoms are at 100%. The past 3 days i've had a big headache, i've felt sick all the time, my IBS has been playing up, i've been getting chest pain, i've been getting chest tremors, i've been getting shortness of breath but usually i've always got a reason like I think \xa0yeah to be fair I have been stressed lately, but I haven't..\xa0 I know that anxiety has a mind of it's own but I cant get my head round it, im not stressed but like this morning I was sat at my desk and had, what i think, was a miniature panic attack, chest tremor and breath went shallow for a split second then it went. If it wasnt the fact I can generally control my panic attacks where I don't panic then I think it would of exploded into something else. Bllllaaaa, woke up today thinking I'm going to push through but then that little panic attack has set me back, only another 8 hours at work sat at a computer..  The only thing I can think of is that for the past 6 weeks i've been on a good eating and excercise plan, I've been biking 60-80 miles a week, I felt great. They say that excercise burns off some of the adrealine so probably why my anxiety was at bay, but for the past week and half i've sacked off the healthy eating and ate like an absolute pig, pizza after pizza, went out all weekend drinking etc.. havent really excercised at all. So i dont know if it's my body just on a bit of a comedown like 'hey we felt great, now your back to your old self, anxietys back too' Maybe because my body was on low anxiety for 6 weeks that now i've been back off it it's hit me harder.\xa0
translated: ကျွန်တော် နားမလည်နိုင်ဘူး။ မကြာသေးမီက စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုတွေ သိပ်မရှိခဲ့ပေမယ့် ဗုဒ္ဓဟူးနေ့ကစပြီး ကျွန်တော့်ရဲ့ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှု လက္ခဏာတွေက ၁၀၀% အထိ ဖြစ်နေပါတယ်။ လွန်ခဲ့တဲ့ ၃ ရက်အတွင်း ခေါင်းကိုက်တာ၊ အမြဲတမ်း နေမကောင်းသလို ခံစားရတာ၊ IBS က ပြန်ပေါ်လာတာ၊ ရင်ဘတ်အောင့်တာ၊ ရင်ဘတ်တုန်တာ၊ အသက်ရှူမဝတာတွေ ဖြစ်နေပါတယ်။ အရင်ကဆို အကြောင်းပြချက်တစ်ခုခု ရှိတတ်ပါတယ်။ ဥပမာ - စိတ်ဖိစီးနေလို့လို့ တွေးမိတတ်ပေမယ့် အခုတော့ မဟုတ်ဘူး။ စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်မှုက သူ့ဘာသာသူ ဖြစ်ပေါ်နိုင်တယ်ဆိုတာ သိပေမယ့် ကျွန်တော် နားမလည်နိုင်ဘူး...

original: I've been on sertraline for 3 weeks, 1 week 50mg and 1 weeks 100mg. I can't see any difference yet so I phoned my GP who said I could go up to 150mg if I wanted and that it can take a while to take effect. Does anyone else have any experience of sertraline and when it worked for them ?? Also how do you know when your improving ??? Feeling very low today. Thank you
translated: ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မဟာ Sertraline ကို ၃ ပတ်သောက်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ပထမ ၁ ပတ်ကို 50mg နဲ့ နောက် ၁ ပတ်ကို 100mg သောက်ခဲ့ပါတယ်။ ဘာမှထူးခြားတာ မတွေ့သေးလို့ ဆရာဝန်ကို ဖုန်းဆက်တော့ ဆရာဝန်က လိုအပ်ရင် 150mg ထိ တိုးသောက်လို့ရပြီး အာနိသင်ပြဖို့ အချိန်ယူရတယ်လို့ ပြောပါတယ်။ Sertraline သောက်ဖူးတဲ့သူတွေ ဘယ်အချိန်မှာ အာနိသင်ပြလဲဆိုတာ သိပါသလား။ ပြီးတော့ တိုးတက်လာပြီဆိုတာကို ဘယ်လိုသိနိုင်မလဲ။ ဒီနေ့ အရမ်းစိတ်ဓာတ်ကျနေပါတယ်။ ကျေးဇူးတင်ပါတယ်။

translated: ဟိုင်းရှင့်၊ မနေ့ညက အရမ်းဆိုးရွားခဲ့လို့ ဒီည ဆေးထပ်သောက်ရမှာကို ကြောက်နေတယ်။ ပထမ ၈ ရက်ကို ၅ မီလီဂရမ်နဲ့စပြီး အခု ၁၀ မီလီဂရမ်ကို ၁၆ ရက်မြောက် သောက်နေတာပါ။ မအိပ်နိုင်ဘူး၊ မနေ့ညက အရမ်းစိတ်လှုပ်ရှားနေတယ်။ မယုံနိုင်လောက်အောင် ကြောက်လန့်နေတယ်။ ကျွန်မကို ငြိမ်သက်အောင် ၂ မီလီဂရမ် ဒိုင်ယာဇီပမ် (Diazepam) သောက်လိုက်ရလို့ နောက်ဆုံးတော့ အိပ်ပျော်သွားတယ်... ဝိုး! မနက်မှာ အရမ်းနေမကောင်းဘူး။ ညမှာ တစ်ဝက်နဲ့ မနက်မှာ တစ်ဝက်သောက်ရင် အဆင်ပြေနိုင်မလား။ ဘာများ အကြံပေးနိုင်မလဲရှင့် x

original: Hi all,-- -- Sorry for the double post this morning. I think I have posted that I have been having cognitive issues- word recall, can't remember if I took my meds, if I look at a calendar I can't figure out the day of the week etc...-- -- My Primary put in a referral to a Neuro, and I have called every day for 2 weeks with no response, and My rheumy put in a referral for a NeuroPsych exam, but there is a long waiting list and I have not even been called yet for an appt.-- -- Lately cognitive issues are much worse. I get in the car and I have to think, do I need N, D, R and mentally focus hard to remember neutral Drive reverse.. I turn on my computer and It takes 30-45 seconds for me to remember how to get online. I am getting lost in parking lots at places I shop regularly and I always park in the same section.-- -- I no I need to call my Primary today and let her know that the local Neuro has not called back...I think I would be happy at this point if someone just ordered an MRI to be on the safe side. -- -- This feels very different than the Brain fog I used to experience and to the other experiences I have heard described here and in the Fibro room.-- -- I already knew I lost my mind psychologically, looks like the other half is going to! -- Thanks, LuLu
translated: အားလုံး မင်္ဂလာပါ။ ယနေ့မနက်တွင် နှစ်ခါတင်မိသည့်အတွက် တောင်းပန်ပါသည်။ ကျွန်ုပ်သည် မှတ်ဉာဏ်ဆိုင်ရာပြဿနာများ (cognitive issues) ရှိနေကြောင်း တင်ခဲ့ဖူးသည်ဟု ထင်ပါသည်။ စကားလုံးများ ပြန်ခေါ်နိုင်စွမ်းမရှိခြင်း၊ ဆေးသောက်ပြီးပြီလား မမှတ်မိခြင်း၊ ပြက္ခဒိန်ကို ကြည့်လျှင် ရက်သတ္တပတ်၏နေ့ကို မတွက်နိုင်ခြင်း စသည်တို့ဖြစ်သည်။ ကျွန်ုပ်၏ မူလဆရာဝန် (Primary) က အာရုံကြောအထူးကုဆရာဝန် (Neuro) ထံ လွှဲပေးခဲ့ပြီး တုံ့ပြန်မှုမရှိသဖြင့် ၂ ပတ်ကြာ နေ့တိုင်း ဖုန်းခေါ်ခဲ့သည်။ ကျွန်ုပ်၏ အဆစ်အမြစ်ရောင်ရောဂါအထူးကုဆရာဝန် (rheumy) က အာရုံကြောဆိုင်ရာ စိတ်ပညာစစ်ဆေးမှု (NeuroPsych exam) အတွက် လွှဲပေးခဲ့သော်လည်း စောင့်ဆိုင်းရမည့်စာရင်းမှာ ရှည်လျားပြီး ရက်ချိန်းအတွက်ပင် ယခုထိ ဖုန်းခေါ်ဆိုခြင်း မခံရသေးပါ။ မကြာသေးမီက မှတ်ဉာဏ်ဆိုင်ရာပြဿနာများ...

original: Before May of this year I was a happy, perfectly normal 29 year old. I was feeling stressed at work for a while, but then all the sudden one night in May I began worrying about my teeth and skin, sure I was going to die of cancer from one of them. Of course this is irrational, everything turned out  fine. But I feel like every since then my brain literally cleared out everything else from my job skills to my favorite books. I started not sleeping at all and got anxious all the time even after I realized how irrational the thoughts were. But no matter what I did after that all I could think about was/is anxiety. Now it's taken over my whole life and I haven't slept in 4 months. I tried antidepressants and sleeping pills which did nothing. All I can think about is this time the point where I feel like I will die of no sleep. Everyone says just forget about it. My question is, can you really forget everything if you convinced yourself you were dying? Nobody understands this and I want to get better but they keep telling me the Forgetting is all in my head and I just have to move on and focus on other things. I can only speak and write well about this stuff. I have a bachelor's degree in photography and a minor in writing. I love to we write and be creative right now I feel like I can barely make a sandwich is is too late with all this sleep loss to get my skills/memory back? Everyone around me says yes but idk if they understand completely.
translated: ဒီနှစ် မေလမတိုင်ခင်က ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မဟာ ပျော်ရွှင်ပြီး ပုံမှန် ၂၉ နှစ်အရွယ် လူတစ်ယောက်ပါ။ အလုပ်မှာ စိတ်ဖိစီးမှုတွေ ခံစားနေရတာ ခဏလောက်ရှိပြီ။ ဒါပေမဲ့ မေလထဲက တစ်ညမှာ ရုတ်တရက် ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ သွားနဲ့ အသားအရေကို စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်လာပြီး အဲဒီတစ်ခုခုကနေ ကင်ဆာရောဂါနဲ့ သေတော့မယ်လို့ ထင်လာတယ်။ ဒါတွေက အဓိပ္ပာယ်မရှိဘူးဆိုတာ သိပါတယ်။ အရာအားလုံးက အဆင်ပြေသွားပါတယ်။ ဒါပေမဲ့ အဲဒီအချိန်ကစပြီး ကျွန်တော့်/ကျွန်မရဲ့ ဦးနှောက်ထဲက အလုပ်နဲ့ပတ်သက်တဲ့ ကျွမ်းကျင်မှုတွေကအစ အကြိုက်ဆုံးစာအုပ်တွေအဆုံး အားလုံးရှင်းထုတ်လိုက်သလို ခံစားရတယ်။ လုံးဝအိပ်မပျော်တော့ဘဲ အမြဲတမ်း စိုးရိမ်ပူပန်နေခဲ့တယ်။ အဲဒီနောက်ပိုင်းမှာလည်း...
